caption:
The protein conversation is louder than ever—and more confusing.

The old recommended dietary allowance (RDA) was about survival, not strength, metabolism, or healthy aging.

Today’s science points to higher ranges for many people.
The right amount? It depends on you: your age, activity, goals, and health status.

Swipe to slide 5 for a protein cheat sheet you’ll want for later.

Not medical advice. Consult a medical professional with any medical questions. For marketing purposes only.

caption:
Is your book club the secret to better living?

caption:
Until 1993, her medicine was only tested on him.

Today, women are still:
- At higher risk of delayed or missed diagnoses than men.
- Waiting longer for pain medication. 
- 50% more likely to be misdiagnosed during a heart attack. 

And the list goes on.
But she isn’t just a number.

She’s your mom, your partner, your sister.
She innovates. She creates. She heals.
She builds companies and communities.
And whether she’s changing the entire world, or just yours, her health matters.

having a puerto rican phone number excludes me from function healrh even when ive lived in california for yearsss. please reconsider this! i’m so bummed i can’t participate in this program just because of a phone number :(

And the downstream effect is still being felt. Most hormone reference ranges used in labs today were established from predominantly male study populations. So when a woman's ferritin, testosterone, or thyroid markers are evaluated against those ranges, the "normal" flag may mean nothing about what is actually optimal for her biology. 1993 was a start, but the lab ranges haven't fully caught up.

Two problems with FunctionHealth. Their analysis and customer support is heavily AI based. They also renew your account without telling you the money is coming out. Good luck trying to speak to an actual person.
And why did you exclude progesterone from your annual test? It is only available as an add-on. For menopausal women that is important to check.

**yt women. Black and brown women are still excluded from much of current testing, and there are significant disparities between treatment and health outcomes that need to be addressed. We still have so far to go.

caption:
HbA1c is your 3-month blood sugar average.

Glucose sticks to your red blood cells and stays there. The higher your blood sugar, the more sticks.

What can help lower it? Consider:
 • Eating in an 8-hour window
 • Starting with fiber and protein
 • Taking short walks after meals

Consistency matters. 

Test HbA1c with Function and see patterns, not snapshots.
firstComment:
Living with diabetes for 10 years now and wow does it make a difference in how you eat and what you do afterwards. More fibre and veg with meals, and eat the veg first, then your protein and starch/carbs last to slow absorbing in blood stream and prevent spikes. 
Go for a walk after meals. Even just 15 minutes. I walk my dog and it helps me get gas out and prevents me from getting sleepy after a meal. 
Recently I heard simply flexing your muscles like little calf raises while under your desk can help your body burn sugars. But I love my walks, rain or shine.

A1c can be false low. Short life of red blood cell. Or due to the reactive hypoglycemia - glucose dipping too low and giving lower numbers as 3 months average. CGM and fasting insulin are the best way to actually understand your glucose metabolism.
